name: Flávio Dias
title: Mechanical Engineer | Maintenance & Reliability | Maintenance Planning and Control (PCM) | Asset Management (CAPEX/OPEX) | Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) | Continuous Improvement (PDCA/DMAIC)
summary: Mechanical Engineer with experience in maintenance, reliability, and asset management in high-criticality industrial environments. Strong cross-functional work with operations, maintenance, quality, and vendors/contractors, using data-driven decision-making to improve operational stability. Led an annual CAPEX (Capital Expenditure) program of BRL 8 million, implemented MES (Manufacturing Execution System), and built KPIs (Key Performance Indicators) for traceability and control. Hands-on background with maintenance performance metrics (OEE, MTBF, MTTR), standardization, failure investigation (RCA), and continuous improvement (PDCA/DMAIC). Key differentiators: international technical representation presenting an end-to-end case (diagnosis -> solution -> results), and a Qualis A2 (Springer) publication on gear vibration mitigation, directly applicable to rotating equipment and reliability. Transitioning into vessel maintenance and critical assets, with a structured ramp-up plan for NORMAM (Brazilian Maritime Authority Standards), ISM Code (International Safety Management Code), ISPS Code (International Ship and Port Facility Security Code), and Class Society interface.
subsummary: Maintenance & Reliability - Maintenance Planning and Control (PCM) - Asset Management - CAPEX/OPEX - Contractors - KPIs (OEE/MTBF/MTTR) - Root Cause Analysis (RCA) - Vibration/Rotating Equipment

experience.title: Industrial Engineering Specialist (Operations-Maintenance-Tooling Interface)
tasks: Functional lead for an annual CAPEX (Capital Expenditure) program of BRL 8 million, including technical justification, scope definition, prioritization, supplier selection, schedule control, and engineering validation.
On-site shopfloor (Gemba) work diagnosing recurring issues, defining corrective actions, and standardizing practices to stabilize operations and reduce losses.
Implemented and acted as MES (Manufacturing Execution System) key-user for parameter monitoring and traceability, increasing operational visibility and supporting RCA (Root Cause Analysis).
Built KPIs (Key Performance Indicators) and reports in Excel and Power BI to track performance, variability, and efficiency, enabling criticality-based prioritization.
Technical interface with maintenance, production, quality, and vendors/contractors to ensure execution quality, safety, and on-time delivery.
International technical representation presenting an end-to-end case (diagnosis -> solution -> results) on MES integration and operational stability at an overseas conference.

experience.title: Industrial Analyst - Welding & Stamping
tasks: Supported welding and stamping operations with process diagnostics, defect analysis, and corrective actions.
Applied PDCA/DMAIC to eliminate recurring issues and improve process reliability and stability.
Standardized documentation and quality controls to support ISO 9001 compliance.

experience.title: Mechanical Engineering Intern
tasks: Restructured the Mechanical Systems and Control Laboratory with improvements in layout, infrastructure, and safety.
Implemented 5S and authored a best-practices manual to improve operational accessibility and consistency.
Supported the lab physical expansion and enabled simultaneous experiments.

experience.title: Teaching Assistant - Mechanical Vibrations
tasks: Supported vibration measurements using accelerometers and DAQ (Data Acquisition) systems.
Guided students in FFT (Fast Fourier Transform), signal interpretation, and correlation between experimental data and analytical models.
Applied fundamentals to rotating equipment vibration diagnostics and reliability concepts.

experience.title: Maintenance Engineering Intern (Reliability)
tasks: Developed reliability dashboards (OEE, MTBF, MTTR) integrating maintenance and production data.
Supported preventive maintenance planning and equipment reliability analysis.
Created maintenance manuals and inspection checklists to improve standardization and diagnostic reliability.

comments: Focus areas: strength of materials, mechanical vibrations, machine elements, modeling, analysis, and control of dynamic systems.
Research in gear vibrations with a Qualis A2 (Springer) publication.
Hands-on training in Lean Six Sigma, Scrum, Excel, Power BI, and Minitab applied to a real cost-reduction and process-optimization project.
Project goal: reduce lead time by 20% in an automotive supplier; outcome: +33% production capacity and >200k/year financial return.

skills: Maintenance KPIs (Key Performance Indicators): OEE, MTBF, MTTR
Root Cause Analysis (RCA) and Failure Analysis
FFT (Fast Fourier Transform) and DAQ (Data Acquisition)
Advanced Excel, Power BI, and Minitab

CS50: Introduction to Computer Science with Python

languages: English: Fluent (C2 self-taught; full professional proficiency)
